chunks.text: Session identifiers MUST be generated using a cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator (CSPRNG). Math.random(), rand(), random.randint MUST NOT be used.
Session IDs MUST have at least 128 bits of entropy.
Session IDs MUST be at least 32 characters long when hex-encoded, 22 characters when base64.
Session IDs MUST be transmitted only in HTTP cookies. URL parameters and hidden form fields PROHIBITED.
Session IDs MUST NOT appear in URLs, query strings, or referrer headers.
Session cookies MUST have the Secure attribute set. Transmission over unencrypted HTTP PROHIBITED.
Session cookies MUST have the HttpOnly attribute set to prevent client-side script access.
Session cookies MUST set SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict. SameSite=None requires justification.
Cookie Domain attribute MUST be set to the most restrictive domain possible. Wildcard domains PROHIBITED.
Cookie Path attribute MUST be set to the most specific path possible.
A new session ID MUST be issued upon successful authentication. Pre-authentication session IDs MUST be invalidated.
Session regeneration MUST occur before associating the session with authenticated user data.
On logout, the server MUST invalidate the session on the server side and clear the session cookie on the client.
Logout MUST invalidate all server-side session data, not just remove the cookie.
All existing sessions MUST be invalidated when a user changes their password, except the current session.
Systems SHOULD limit concurrent sessions per user. Exceeding the limit MUST either deny new sessions or invalidate the oldest.
Sessions MUST expire after a configured idle period. Default: 15 minutes for sensitive, 30 minutes for standard applications.
Idle timeout MUST be enforced server-side. Client-side timeout alone is insufficient.
Sessions MUST have an absolute maximum lifetime regardless of activity. Default: 8 hours for standard, 1 hour for sensitive.
Session data stored server-side MUST be protected from unauthorized access. Encryption at rest REQUIRED for sensitive session data.
Sessions SHOULD be bound to client characteristics (IP, User-Agent). Changes SHOULD trigger re-authentication.
Remember-me tokens MUST be cryptographically random, single-use, and stored hashed server-side.
Remember-me tokens MUST have a maximum lifetime and MUST be invalidated on password change.
API sessions MUST use token-based authentication (JWT, OAuth2) rather than cookie-based sessions where possible.
All state-changing operations MUST be protected against CSRF using synchronizer tokens, double-submit cookies, or SameSite cookies.
CSRF tokens MUST be unique per session and cryptographically random.
CSRF tokens MUST be validated server-side for every state-changing request.
Server-side session stores MUST use authenticated encryption. Plaintext session storage PROHIBITED.
Session stores MUST be isolated per application in shared hosting environments.
Session creation, destruction, timeout, and invalidation events MUST be logged with timestamp and user identifier.
Failed session validation attempts MUST be logged and monitored for session hijacking detection.
Session logs MUST NOT contain session IDs or tokens. Use opaque references.
Session anomaly detection SHOULD alert on unusual patterns (rapid creation, geographic impossibility).
All passwords MUST be hashed using Argon2id (preferred), bcrypt, or scrypt. MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256 (unsalted), and custom hashing are PROHIBITED.
Password hashing MUST use a unique random salt per password of at least 16 bytes.
Argon2id parameters MUST use minimum: memory 64MB, iterations 3, parallelism 1. Bcrypt minimum cost factor 12.
Credentials MUST only be transmitted over TLS 1.2+. HTTP transmission of passwords PROHIBITED.
Passwords MUST be sent in the request body, never in URL parameters or headers.
Login endpoints MUST implement rate limiting. Maximum 5 failed attempts per account per 15 minutes.
Account lockout MUST be temporary (minimum 15 minutes) or require manual unlock. Permanent lockout creates denial of service.
MFA MUST be available for all user accounts and REQUIRED for privileged accounts.
MFA recovery codes MUST be single-use, cryptographically random, and stored hashed.
Minimum password length MUST be 12 characters. Maximum MUST be at least 128 characters.
Passwords MUST be checked against known breached password databases (e.g., HaveIBeenPwned).
Password reset tokens MUST be cryptographically random, single-use, and expire within 1 hour.
Password reset MUST NOT reveal whether an account exists. Use generic messages.
Authentication failure responses MUST NOT distinguish between invalid username and invalid password.
Authentication state MUST be maintained server-side. Client-side authentication state alone is insufficient.
OAuth MUST use Authorization Code flow with PKCE for public clients. Implicit flow PROHIBITED.
OAuth state parameter MUST be used and validated to prevent CSRF.
API keys MUST be transmitted in headers, never in URL parameters.
JWT MUST use RS256 or ES256 algorithms. HS256 with weak secrets and 'none' algorithm PROHIBITED.
JWT MUST validate: signature, expiration (exp), issuer (iss), audience (aud). Clock skew tolerance maximum 30 seconds.
JWT secrets MUST be at least 256 bits for HMAC algorithms. RSA keys minimum 2048 bits.
Plaintext passwords MUST NOT be stored anywhere — database, logs, files, or memory dumps.
Authorization checks MUST be enforced server-side for every request to protected resources.
Default access MUST be deny-all. Access granted only by explicit permission.
RBAC roles MUST follow the principle of least privilege. Overly broad roles PROHIBITED.
Role assignments MUST be auditable and logged.
All data access MUST verify the requesting user has authorization for the specific object. Sequential/guessable IDs MUST be paired with authorization checks.
Users MUST NOT be able to access other users' data by manipulating parameters, IDs, or paths.
Admin functions MUST verify admin role on every request. Client-side role checks are insufficient.
Every API endpoint and controller action MUST enforce authorization. Missing authorization middleware is a CRITICAL finding.
File access MUST validate that resolved paths remain within allowed directories. Path traversal sequences (../) MUST be rejected.
All input MUST be validated server-side. Client-side validation alone is insufficient.
Input validation MUST use allowlists (permitted values) over denylists (blocked values).
Input length MUST be validated against defined maximums for all fields.
All output MUST be encoded for the context (HTML, JavaScript, URL, CSS, SQL) before rendering.
Template engines MUST use auto-escaping by default. Manual safe/raw markers require security review.
HTTP parameter pollution MUST be handled by accepting only the first or last value, never concatenating.
User-supplied filenames MUST be sanitized. Directory traversal, null bytes, and special characters MUST be stripped or rejected.
All queries MUST use parameterized queries or ORM query builders. String concatenation for SQL PROHIBITED.
Dynamic table/column names MUST be validated against an allowlist. Parameterized queries cannot protect identifiers.
OS command execution MUST use parameterized APIs (subprocess with list args). Shell=True PROHIBITED.
User input MUST NEVER be incorporated into OS commands, even with sanitization.
LDAP queries MUST use parameterized filters. String concatenation for LDAP filters PROHIBITED.
Server-side HTTP requests MUST validate destination URLs against an allowlist. Requests to internal/private IPs PROHIBITED.
URL validation MUST resolve DNS and verify the IP is not in private ranges before connecting.
Untrusted data MUST NOT be deserialized using unsafe deserializers (pickle, yaml.load, ObjectInputStream). Use safe alternatives.
If deserialization of complex objects is required, implement strict type allowlists.
XML parsers MUST disable external entity processing and DTD processing.
A Content-Security-Policy header MUST be deployed. unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval MUST NOT be used in script-src.
User input MUST NOT be inserted into the DOM via innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, or eval. Use textContent or safe DOM APIs.
All user input reflected in responses MUST be context-appropriately encoded. URL parameters reflected in HTML MUST be HTML-encoded.
User-generated content stored in databases MUST be encoded on output, not just on input. Both input validation and output encoding REQUIRED.
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff MUST be set on all responses. X-XSS-Protection: 0 MUST be set (defer to CSP).
All API endpoints MUST require authentication except explicitly designated public endpoints.
All API endpoints MUST enforce rate limiting to prevent abuse. Rate limits MUST be per-user or per-API-key.
API endpoints MUST validate request body schema, content type, and parameter types. Unexpected fields MUST be rejected.
APIs MUST be versioned. Deprecated versions MUST have a sunset date and migration path.
API error responses MUST NOT expose internal implementation details, stack traces, or database errors.
GraphQL endpoints MUST disable introspection in production and enforce query depth/complexity limits.
Symmetric encryption MUST use AES-256 (GCM mode preferred). DES, 3DES, RC4, Blowfish PROHIBITED.
Encryption keys MUST be managed through a key management service. Hardcoded keys PROHIBITED.
Cryptographic hashing MUST use SHA-256 or SHA-3. MD5 and SHA-1 PROHIBITED for security purposes.
All connections MUST use TLS 1.2 or higher. SSLv3, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1 PROHIBITED.

Secrets MUST be stored in approved vaults (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault). Plaintext secrets in code, config files, VCS, environment variable files, and properties files PROHIBITED.
Security-sensitive random values MUST use cryptographic RNG (secrets module, SecureRandom). Math.random(), random module without secrets PROHIBITED.
TLS certificate validation MUST NOT be disabled. verify=False, InsecureSkipVerify, NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 PROHIBITED.
Production error responses MUST NOT expose stack traces, SQL queries, internal paths, or framework details. Generic error messages only.
Custom error pages MUST be configured for all HTTP error codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 500).
All exceptions MUST be caught and handled. Unhandled exceptions MUST NOT propagate to users.
Empty catch/except blocks PROHIBITED. All exceptions MUST be logged.
Authentication events (login, logout, failed attempts, lockouts) MUST be logged with timestamp, user ID, IP, and outcome.
Authorization failures (access denied) MUST be logged with the resource, action, and user.
Logs MUST NOT contain passwords, tokens, session IDs, credit card numbers, SSNs, or other sensitive data.
Log injection MUST be prevented by sanitizing user input before logging. Newlines and control characters MUST be stripped.
HSTS header MUST be set with max-age >= 31536000 (1 year). includeSubDomains RECOMMENDED.
CSP header MUST be deployed on all pages. default-src 'self' as baseline.
X-Frame-Options MUST be set to DENY or SAMEORIGIN to prevent clickjacking.
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff MUST be set on all responses.
Referrer-Policy MUST be set to strict-origin-when-cross-origin or more restrictive.
Permissions-Policy MUST restrict access to sensitive browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation) to same-origin.
Access-Control-Allow-Origin MUST NOT use wildcard (*) for authenticated endpoints. Specific origins only.
CORS credentials (Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true) MUST NOT be combined with wildcard origin.
CORS preflight responses MUST validate the Origin header against an allowlist.
Uploaded files MUST be validated by magic bytes (file signature), not just file extension.
File extensions MUST be validated against an allowlist. Executable extensions (.exe, .sh, .bat, .php, .jsp) PROHIBITED.
Uploaded files MUST be stored outside the web root with randomly generated names. Original filenames MUST NOT be used for storage.
File upload size MUST be limited server-side. Maximum size varies by use case but MUST be explicitly configured.
Uploaded images MUST be re-processed (re-encoded) to strip metadata and prevent image-based attacks.
All dependencies MUST be pinned to exact versions in production. Ranges (^, ~, >=) PROHIBITED in lockfiles.
Dependencies MUST be scanned for known vulnerabilities before deployment. CI/CD MUST fail on critical/high CVEs.
Dependencies MUST be sourced from verified registries. Lockfiles MUST include integrity hashes.
All data MUST be classified by sensitivity (public, internal, confidential, restricted). Handling rules MUST match classification.
Confidential and restricted data MUST be encrypted at rest using AES-256.
All data in transit MUST be encrypted using TLS 1.2+. Unencrypted HTTP for sensitive data PROHIBITED.
Applications MUST only collect and retain data that is necessary. Excessive data collection PROHIBITED.
Data retention periods MUST be defined and enforced. Expired data MUST be securely deleted.
PII MUST NOT appear in logs, URLs, cookies, or client-side storage. Display MUST be masked; full reveal only on explicit logged action. Bulk export requires elevated authorization and logging.
Pre-authentication session data MUST NOT be trusted after authentication. Session data migration requires explicit validation.
Logout MUST clear all related tokens including refresh tokens, remember-me tokens, and API tokens.
Session timeout warnings SHOULD be displayed to users before automatic logout.
Absolute timeout MUST be enforced independently of idle timeout. Both MUST be applied.
Session data MUST be validated for integrity on every request. Tamper detection REQUIRED for client-side session stores.
Token binding changes MUST invalidate the session, not just log a warning.
API tokens MUST have scoped permissions. Full-access tokens for limited operations PROHIBITED.
CSRF protection MUST be applied to all forms and AJAX requests that modify state.
Session stores MUST implement automatic expiration. Orphaned sessions MUST be cleaned up.
Concurrent session violations MUST be logged and alerted.
Session store failures MUST trigger alerts and graceful degradation, not silent data loss.
CAPTCHA or progressive delays MUST be implemented after repeated failed login attempts.
Password composition rules SHOULD NOT restrict character sets. All Unicode characters MUST be allowed.
Password reset flows MUST invalidate all existing sessions after successful password change.
OAuth redirect URIs MUST be validated against a strict allowlist. Open redirects PROHIBITED.
JWT refresh tokens MUST have bounded lifetime and MUST be rotated on use. Reuse of consumed refresh tokens MUST invalidate the entire token family.
Temporary credentials in memory MUST be zeroed after use. Garbage collection alone is insufficient.
Symbolic links MUST be resolved and validated. Symlink-based path traversal MUST be prevented.
XML parsers MUST disable XInclude processing and external parameter entities.
Regular expressions used for input validation MUST be tested for ReDoS vulnerability. Catastrophic backtracking patterns PROHIBITED.
